title: AlUla Part 2: A Graveyard of Empires
_note: PART 2 — fresh angle vs Part 1. NO RECITAL: Qur'an appears only as on-screen Arabic + English (synced); voice speaks English only. Brand theme 'ketabi' (deep green + gold + cream — no blue). Every beat has a distinct animated Remotion scene (dunes|geo|rays|stone|nightsky|embers) or a map, so it's visual-after-visual, not flat. TTS: AlUla='al-OO-la', al-Hijr='al-HIJR', Thamud='tha-MOOD', Dadan='da-DAN', Lihyan='LIH-yan', Nabataeans='nab-a-TEE-ans'. ACCURACY: Thamud 'carving homes in the mountains' is the Qur'anic statement (15:82); the Hegra TOMBS are credited to the Nabataeans (beat 11). For #HistoryTikTokContest (1min+).

segments.text: This valley in Saudi Arabia hides a secret almost no tourist is ever told.
segments.caption: AlUla

segments.text: It isn't one lost city. It's four. Stacked on the very same sand.
segments.caption: Four lost civilizations

segments.text: And the Quran named the most terrifying of them first.
This is al-Hijr, the land of Thamud. They carved entire homes into solid mountains, then dared their own prophet to bring God's punishment down on them.
segments.caption: Quran · Surah Al-Hijr 15:80-84

segments.text: He warned them. They refused. And a single shriek left their cliffs standing empty forever.
segments.caption: Surah Al-Hijr 15:83

segments.text: But why did empire after empire bleed for this one patch of desert?
segments.caption: The incense road

segments.text: Because it sat on the incense road, the richest trade route in the ancient world.
Frankincense. Myrrh. Gold. Whoever held this oasis taxed all of it.
So they came. First the kingdom of Dadan, already powerful nearly three thousand years ago.
segments.caption: Kingdom of Dadan

segments.text: Then mighty Lihyan, whose giant stone statues are still being pulled out of the sand today.
segments.caption: Lihyanite kingdom

segments.text: Then the Nabataeans, the builders of Petra. They cut the tombs every influencer films today, certain that's the whole story.
segments.caption: Nabataeans · Hegra

segments.text: Four powers. One valley. Each certain it could never fall. Every one of them is now silent stone.
And the Quran tells us exactly what to do with a place like this. Travel the earth, and see how the story of those before you ended.
segments.caption: Quran · Surah Ar-Rum 30:42

segments.text: AlUla was never a luxury escape. It's a graveyard of empires, left standing as a sign for anyone willing to learn.
segments.caption: A sign for those who reflect

sources: Qur'an, Surah Al-Hijr 15:80-84 — the companions of al-Hijr (Thamud) denied the messengers; 15:82 they carved homes in the mountains; 15:83 'the shriek (sayhah) seized them at morning.' (also Surah Al-A'raf 7:73-79.)
Dadan (Dedan): ancient oasis kingdom at al-Khuraybah, AlUla; major caravan-trade power in the early-mid 1st millennium BCE.
Lihyan (Lihyanite kingdom): c. 5th-2nd century BCE; one of the largest Arabian kingdoms; monumental Lihyanite statues excavated at Dadan.

Nabataeans (1st c. BCE - 1st c. CE): builders of Petra; carved the monumental tombs of Hegra (Mada'in Salih) at AlUla. The famous tombs are NABATAEAN, not Thamud's — kept distinct in the script (beat 11 vs beat 4).
Incense road: AlUla/Dadan was a key station on the route carrying frankincense and myrrh from southern Arabia north to the Mediterranean.
Qur'an, Surah Ar-Rum 30:42 — 'Travel through the land and observe how was the end of those before' (shown as Arabic + English on screen; NOT recited).

ACCURACY NOTE: dating of Qur'anic Thamud is debated, so the script does not force a strict chronology; it presents the Qur'anic and archaeological records together. The false 'ozone hole over AlUla' claim is deliberately excluded.
